However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]

(Containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|Tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}) |excavated_burial goods = Human remains, amber pillow, tri-colored lidded inkstone, glass writing tube |satellite_kofun = |built = Mid-7th century |interred_persons = One young male
(Hypothesis: a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family) |mausoleum = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}) |notable_features = Mound no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tatsuta Goboyama
No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]

No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}}

No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun |image = [[File:竜田御坊山3号墳 横口式石槨.JPG|280px]]

Exhibit at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum)}} |alternate_name = Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun | part_of = Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan |coordinates = 34.611361 |coordinates = 135.726536 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |size = Diameter 8m
Height 2.5m |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}
(Containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|Tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}) |excavated_burial goods = Human remains, amber pillow, tri-colored lidded inkstone, glass writing tube |satellite_kofun = |built = Mid-7th century |interred_persons = One young male
(Hypothesis: a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family) |mausoleum = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}) |notable_features = Mound no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tatsuta Goboyama
No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

(Hypothesis: a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family) |mausoleum = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}) |notable_features = Mound no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tatsuta Goboyama
No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] {{Kofun navbar}}

No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}

Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}

The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Important designation1 (Japan)]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}
