So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun |image = [[File:竜田御坊山3号墳 横口式石槨.JPG|280px]]
{{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}:附 Historic Site1)
{{small|(Left: lid and base stones, right: sealing stone. Exhibit at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum)}} |alternate_name = Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun | part_of = Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan |coordinates = 34.611361 |coordinates = 135.726536 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |size = Diameter 8m
Height 2.5m |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}
(Containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|Tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}) |excavated_burial goods = Human remains, amber pillow, tri-colored lidded inkstone, glass writing tube |satellite_kofun = |built = Mid-7th century |interred_persons = One young male
(Hypothesis: a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family) |mausoleum = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}) |notable_features = Mound no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tatsuta Goboyama
No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Important designation1 (Japan)]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun |image = [[File:竜田御坊山3号墳 横口式石槨.JPG|280px]]
{{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} (National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}:附 Historic Site1)
{{small|(Left: lid and base stones, right: sealing stone. Exhibit at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum)}} |alternate_name = Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun | part_of = Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan |coordinates = 34.611361 |coordinates = 135.726536 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |size = Diameter 8m
Height 2.5m |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}
(Containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|Tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}) |excavated_burial goods = Human remains, amber pillow, tri-colored lidded inkstone, glass writing tube |satellite_kofun = |built = Mid-7th century |interred_persons = One young male
(Hypothesis: a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family) |mausoleum = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}) |notable_features = Mound no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tatsuta Goboyama
No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}

The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]

No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |name = Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun |image = [[File:竜田御坊山3号墳 横口式石槨.JPG|280px]]

{{small|(Left: lid and base stones, right: sealing stone. Exhibit at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}} Museum)}} |alternate_name = Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun | part_of = Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan |coordinates = 34.611361 |coordinates = 135.726536 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |size = Diameter 8m
Height 2.5m |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(Containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|Tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft|12=draft}}) |excavated_burial goods = Human remains, amber pillow, tri-colored lidded inkstone, glass writing tube |satellite_kofun = |built = Mid-7th century |interred_persons = One young male
(Hypothesis: a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family) |mausoleum = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) |notable_features = Mound no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tatsuta Goboyama
No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]

Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=draft|12=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|12=draft|12=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]

The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]

revision.comment: 9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:竜田御坊山3号墳 up to rev 99995437
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 竜田御坊山3号墳 |画像 = [[ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳 横口式石槨.JPG|280px]]
[[横口式石槨]]（国の[[重要文化財]]：附指定）
{{small|（左に蓋石・底石、右に閉塞石。[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館展示）}} |別名 = 竜田御坊山古墳 |所属 = 竜田御坊山古墳群 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]龍田北1丁目（字御坊山） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 36|緯度秒 = 40.90 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 35.53 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径8m
高さ2.5m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]]
（内部に漆塗家形[[陶棺]]） |出土品 = 人骨・琥珀製枕・三彩有蓋円面硯・ガラス製筆管 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = 青年男性1人
（一説）上宮王家一族 |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 竜田御坊山
3号墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''竜田御坊山3号墳'''（たつたごぼうやまさんごうふん、'''竜田御坊山古墳'''）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]龍田北にあった[[古墳]]。竜田御坊山古墳群を構成した古墳の1つ。形状は[[円墳]]。現在では墳丘は失われている。出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]北西部、[[龍田神社]]北方・[[法隆寺]]南西の丘陵南斜面に築造された小円墳である。南西に2基の古墳（1・2号墳）が所在し、竜田御坊山古墳群を形成した。[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に宅地造成工事中に発見され、発掘調査のうえで3基とも消滅している{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 墳形は円形で、直径8m・高さ2.5メートル程度と推定される{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、南方向に開口し、内部に漆塗の家形陶棺が収められた。横口式石槨・漆棺の採用は被葬者の身分の高さを示唆するが、精緻な横口式石槨の一方で棺は急ごしらえの様相を呈する点で特殊な例になる。陶棺内からは人骨1体（14-15歳の青年男性）のほか、副葬品として琥珀製枕・三彩有蓋円面硯・ガラス製筆管が検出されており、いずれも全国で唯一の優品として注目される。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。横口式石槨・漆棺の採用からは被葬者の身分の高さがうかがわれ、日本列島では唯一となる副葬品からは被葬者が貴重な文物を入手できる立場にあったことを示唆する{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、厩戸皇子（[[聖徳太子]]）子孫の上宮王家の一員とする説が挙げられる。上宮王家は、厩戸皇子が[[斑鳩宮]]に移住してから[[山背大兄王]]が自害するまでの間の斑鳩地域の一大勢力であり、[[7世紀]]代の[[法隆寺]]・[[中宮寺]]・[[法起寺]]・[[法輪寺 (奈良県斑鳩町)|法輪寺]]の相次ぐ創建とともに本古墳が築造された点で、斑鳩地域の歴史的動向を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 出土品は[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1964年]]（[[昭和]]39年）、宅地造成工事に伴い1号墳・2号墳が破壊{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 * [[1965年]]（昭和40年）、宅地造成工事中に3号墳の発見、発掘調査（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]、[[1977年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 *: 調査後、墳丘消滅。石槨は大和歴史館前庭に移設{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館の新館開館に伴い、石槨を新館前庭に移設{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）6月9日、出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定。 * [[2015年]]度（[[平成]]27年度）、石槨・陶棺の再調査（奈良県立橿原考古学研究所、[[2021年]]に報告）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳 横口式石槨-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|横口式石槨（蓋石・底石）}}]] [[File:Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石槨パース図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南方向に開口した。石槨は底石・蓋石・閉塞石の3石から構成される。平らな底石の上に内部を刳り抜いた蓋石を被せ、小口に板状の閉塞石を置くことで内部を密閉する。各石の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 * 蓋石：最大長2.86メートル、最大幅1.61メートル、最大高0.93メートル *: 蓋石刳込部：奥行2.16メートル、幅0.66-0.68メートル、高さ0.51-0.52メートル * 底石：最大長2.93メートル、最大幅1.67メートル、最大高0.57メートル * 閉塞石：最大幅1.11メートル、最大高0.90メートル、最大厚0.35メートル 本石槨の類例としては[[鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠]]（[[明日香村]]）があるが、本例は鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠よりもやや小型である{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石槨の石材は[[花崗岩]]{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石槨の内面には漆喰の塗布が認められており、特に蓋石刳込部の全面に漆喰が塗られることから、密閉性を意識したと見られる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。また石槨の構築に際しては、高麗尺（7世紀中葉以前）の使用が推定される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 [[ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳出土 漆塗陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|漆塗陶棺（国の重要文化財）}}{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示。}}]] 石槨内には須恵質（系）四注（寄棟）式家形陶棺が収められる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。棺身・棺蓋から構成され、形状は淀川流域の中でも千里丘陵周辺の陶棺との共通性が指摘される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。陶棺の全面（内面・外面）には漆を塗布するという格式の高さが認められ、全国23例の漆棺（夾紵棺・漆塗木棺・漆塗籠棺など）の中でも漆塗陶棺は本例のみになる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。ただし、棺身と棺蓋は噛み合わずセット関係でない点、棺蓋には大きなひび割れがあり内面・外面には補修のための楔形銅板が取り付けられている点、陶栓の径が蓋の円孔の径よりも小さくかみ合わない点（木屑を詰めて使用か）から、あり合わせの部材で組み合わせたと評価される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。また棺身上端部・棺蓋下端部・脚部が焼成後に大きく削り取られている点からは、石槨内に収めるために大きさの調整がなされたと見られる。これらのことから、被葬者を緊急に埋葬する必要が生じたために本棺を準備した様子が示唆される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。なお、かつて本棺は須恵質陶棺の中では最終段階に位置づけられたが、最終段階とした指標は前述の意図的な造作の結果であると見られ、現在では前段階の型式に位置づけるのが妥当とされる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 陶棺内からは人骨のほか、副葬品として琥珀製枕・三彩有蓋円面硯・ガラス製筆管が検出されている{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。人骨は14-15歳の青年男性とされる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。身長は150センチメートル程度と陶棺よりも大きい点で、棺の急ごしらえの様相を支持する{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石槨俯瞰図 Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|石槨展開図 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品（国の重要文化財）}}{{small|左上の金銅製棺飾金具は1号墳出土品、右上の琥珀製枕は復元品（いずれも重要文化財指定外）。奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] 陶棺から検出された副葬品は次の通り。いずれも日本列島内では類例の無いものになる。 * 琥珀製枕 *: 産地分析では岩手県久慈産と同定される。類例がないため詳らかとしないが、石枕からの系譜を指摘する説が挙げられる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 * 三彩有蓋円面硯 *: 類例がないため詳らかとせず、中国からの搬入品とする説と、朝鮮半島からの搬入品とする説がある。報告書では唐代初頭のものとの類似を指摘する{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 * ガラス製筆管 *: 円面硯とのセット関係から、[[王羲之]]『筆経』に見える筆管とする説があるが、確定的ではない。調査後に李静訓墓（[[608年]]埋葬）で類例が出土している{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳出土 琥珀製枕.JPG|琥珀製枕 ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳出土 琥珀製枕 (復元).JPG|琥珀製枕（復元） ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳出土 三彩有蓋円面硯・ガラス管.JPG|三彩有蓋円面硯・ガラス管 == 被葬者 == 竜田御坊山3号墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、上宮王家の一族に比定する説が挙げられる。上宮王家は、厩戸皇子（[[聖徳太子]]）・[[山背大兄王]]を中心とした一族で、[[推古天皇]]13年（[[601年]]）に厩戸皇子が[[斑鳩宮]]に移住してから[[皇極天皇]]2年（[[643年]]）に山背大兄王が自害するまでの間の斑鳩地域の一大勢力とされる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。『[[上宮聖徳法王帝説]]』では、山背大兄王の滅亡の際には王の子弟ら15人が滅ぼされたという{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 竜田御坊山3号墳を上宮王家の一族に比定する説では、横口式石槨・漆棺・副葬品に見る被葬者の身分の高さや、所在地名の「御坊山」が「御廟山」の転訛と考えられることが根拠として挙げられる{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。築造時期と人骨の年齢観からは、上宮王家一族の中でも特に厩戸皇子の孫世代の可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。ただし漆塗陶棺は急ごしらえの様相を呈することから、緊急的な埋葬に至った背景の解明が今後期待される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 斑鳩地域では、中期古墳の[[斑鳩大塚古墳]]・甲塚古墳、後期古墳の[[藤ノ木古墳]]・仏塚古墳、終末期古墳の[[神代古墳]]などが分布しており、本古墳との系譜関係が注意される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 == 竜田御坊山1・2号墳 == 竜田御坊山1号墳・2号墳は3号墳の南西に所在する。各内容は次の通り。 ; 1号墳 : 2号墳の南15メートルに所在{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。1964年（昭和39年）に宅地造成工事に伴い破壊（調査なし）{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 : 埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]状で、長さ約2メートル・幅約1.7メートルを測ったという。石室内部からは人骨3体のほか金銅装環付六花文座金具1（木棺飾金具か）・鉄釘4が出土している。築造時期は古墳時代終末期の7世紀前半-中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 ; 2号墳 : 1号墳の北15メートル、3号墳の南西30メートルに所在{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。1964年（昭和39年）に宅地造成工事に伴い破壊（調査なし）{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 : 埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]と見られ、[[凝灰岩]]製の組合式[[家形石棺]]片が出土している。小型の石棺で、推定規模は長さ1.82メートル・幅1.15メートルを測る。築造時期は古墳時代終末期の7世紀前半-中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 大和御坊山第三号墳出土品（考古資料） - 内訳は以下。奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館保管。1981年（昭和56年）6月9日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10002|大和御坊山第三号墳出土品}}。 ** 三彩有蓋円面硯 1面 ** 管状ガラス製品 1本 ** 琥珀枕残欠 1箇分 ** 漆塗陶棺 1合 ** （附指定）横口式石槨 1基 == 関連施設 == * [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館（橿原市畝傍町） - 竜田御坊山3号墳の石槨・出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=御坊山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=高島徹|authorlink=|year=1989|chapter=御坊山古墳群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|御坊山古墳群（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=絹畠歩・前田俊雄・北井利幸|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf 竜田御坊山3号墳の再検討と被葬者像]}}|date=2021|publisher=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]|journal=考古學論攷 橿原考古学研究所紀要|volume=|number=44|naid=|pages=1-18|ref={{Harvid|絹畠歩ら|2021}}}} - リンクは奈良県立橿原考古学研究所。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]|author=|year=1977|chapter=|title=竜田御坊山古墳 -付 平野塚穴山古墳-（奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査報告 第32冊）|publisher=奈良県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[神代古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun|竜田御坊山古墳}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10002|大和御坊山第三号墳出土品}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=竜田御坊山古墳群 |1-1=奈良県の古墳 |1-2=斑鳩町の歴史 |1-3=古墳群 }} {{デフォルトソート:たつたこほうやまさんこうふん}} revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[竜田御坊山3号墳]] to [[Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 竜田御坊山3号墳 |画像 = [[ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳 横口式石槨.JPG|280px]]
[[横口式石槨]]（国の[[重要文化財]]：附指定）
{{small|（左に蓋石・底石、右に閉塞石。[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館展示）}} |別名 = 竜田御坊山古墳 |所属 = 竜田御坊山古墳群 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]龍田北1丁目（字御坊山） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 36|緯度秒 = 40.90 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 35.53 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径8m
高さ2.5m |埋葬施設 = [[横口式石槨]]
（内部に漆塗家形[[陶棺]]） |出土品 = 人骨・琥珀製枕・三彩有蓋円面硯・ガラス製筆管 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = 青年男性1人
（一説）上宮王家一族 |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 竜田御坊山
3号墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''竜田御坊山3号墳'''（たつたごぼうやまさんごうふん、'''竜田御坊山古墳'''）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[斑鳩町]]龍田北にあった[[古墳]]。竜田御坊山古墳群を構成した古墳の1つ。形状は[[円墳]]。現在では墳丘は失われている。出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]北西部、[[龍田神社]]北方・[[法隆寺]]南西の丘陵南斜面に築造された小円墳である。南西に2基の古墳（1・2号墳）が所在し、竜田御坊山古墳群を形成した。[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に宅地造成工事中に発見され、発掘調査のうえで3基とも消滅している{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 墳形は円形で、直径8m・高さ2.5メートル程度と推定される{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。埋葬施設は[[横口式石槨]]で、南方向に開口し、内部に漆塗の家形陶棺が収められた。横口式石槨・漆棺の採用は被葬者の身分の高さを示唆するが、精緻な横口式石槨の一方で棺は急ごしらえの様相を呈する点で特殊な例になる。陶棺内からは人骨1体（14-15歳の青年男性）のほか、副葬品として琥珀製枕・三彩有蓋円面硯・ガラス製筆管が検出されており、いずれも全国で唯一の優品として注目される。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。横口式石槨・漆棺の採用からは被葬者の身分の高さがうかがわれ、日本列島では唯一となる副葬品からは被葬者が貴重な文物を入手できる立場にあったことを示唆する{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、厩戸皇子（[[聖徳太子]]）子孫の上宮王家の一員とする説が挙げられる。上宮王家は、厩戸皇子が[[斑鳩宮]]に移住してから[[山背大兄王]]が自害するまでの間の斑鳩地域の一大勢力であり、[[7世紀]]代の[[法隆寺]]・[[中宮寺]]・[[法起寺]]・[[法輪寺 (奈良県斑鳩町)|法輪寺]]の相次ぐ創建とともに本古墳が築造された点で、斑鳩地域の歴史的動向を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 出土品は[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1964年]]（[[昭和]]39年）、宅地造成工事に伴い1号墳・2号墳が破壊{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 * [[1965年]]（昭和40年）、宅地造成工事中に3号墳の発見、発掘調査（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]、[[1977年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 *: 調査後、墳丘消滅。石槨は大和歴史館前庭に移設{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館の新館開館に伴い、石槨を新館前庭に移設{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）6月9日、出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定。 * [[2015年]]度（[[平成]]27年度）、石槨・陶棺の再調査（奈良県立橿原考古学研究所、[[2021年]]に報告）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳 横口式石槨-2.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|横口式石槨（蓋石・底石）}}]] [[File:Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石槨パース図}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横口式石槨]]が構築されており、南方向に開口した。石槨は底石・蓋石・閉塞石の3石から構成される。平らな底石の上に内部を刳り抜いた蓋石を被せ、小口に板状の閉塞石を置くことで内部を密閉する。各石の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 * 蓋石：最大長2.86メートル、最大幅1.61メートル、最大高0.93メートル *: 蓋石刳込部：奥行2.16メートル、幅0.66-0.68メートル、高さ0.51-0.52メートル * 底石：最大長2.93メートル、最大幅1.67メートル、最大高0.57メートル * 閉塞石：最大幅1.11メートル、最大高0.90メートル、最大厚0.35メートル 本石槨の類例としては[[鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠]]（[[明日香村]]）があるが、本例は鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠よりもやや小型である{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石槨の石材は[[花崗岩]]{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石槨の内面には漆喰の塗布が認められており、特に蓋石刳込部の全面に漆喰が塗られることから、密閉性を意識したと見られる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。また石槨の構築に際しては、高麗尺（7世紀中葉以前）の使用が推定される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 [[ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳出土 漆塗陶棺.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|漆塗陶棺（国の重要文化財）}}{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示。}}]] 石槨内には須恵質（系）四注（寄棟）式家形陶棺が収められる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。棺身・棺蓋から構成され、形状は淀川流域の中でも千里丘陵周辺の陶棺との共通性が指摘される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。陶棺の全面（内面・外面）には漆を塗布するという格式の高さが認められ、全国23例の漆棺（夾紵棺・漆塗木棺・漆塗籠棺など）の中でも漆塗陶棺は本例のみになる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。ただし、棺身と棺蓋は噛み合わずセット関係でない点、棺蓋には大きなひび割れがあり内面・外面には補修のための楔形銅板が取り付けられている点、陶栓の径が蓋の円孔の径よりも小さくかみ合わない点（木屑を詰めて使用か）から、あり合わせの部材で組み合わせたと評価される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。また棺身上端部・棺蓋下端部・脚部が焼成後に大きく削り取られている点からは、石槨内に収めるために大きさの調整がなされたと見られる。これらのことから、被葬者を緊急に埋葬する必要が生じたために本棺を準備した様子が示唆される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。なお、かつて本棺は須恵質陶棺の中では最終段階に位置づけられたが、最終段階とした指標は前述の意図的な造作の結果であると見られ、現在では前段階の型式に位置づけるのが妥当とされる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 陶棺内からは人骨のほか、副葬品として琥珀製枕・三彩有蓋円面硯・ガラス製筆管が検出されている{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。人骨は14-15歳の青年男性とされる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。身長は150センチメートル程度と陶棺よりも大きい点で、棺の急ごしらえの様相を支持する{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石槨俯瞰図 Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|石槨展開図 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品（国の重要文化財）}}{{small|左上の金銅製棺飾金具は1号墳出土品、右上の琥珀製枕は復元品（いずれも重要文化財指定外）。奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] 陶棺から検出された副葬品は次の通り。いずれも日本列島内では類例の無いものになる。 * 琥珀製枕 *: 産地分析では岩手県久慈産と同定される。類例がないため詳らかとしないが、石枕からの系譜を指摘する説が挙げられる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 * 三彩有蓋円面硯 *: 類例がないため詳らかとせず、中国からの搬入品とする説と、朝鮮半島からの搬入品とする説がある。報告書では唐代初頭のものとの類似を指摘する{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 * ガラス製筆管 *: 円面硯とのセット関係から、[[王羲之]]『筆経』に見える筆管とする説があるが、確定的ではない。調査後に李静訓墓（[[608年]]埋葬）で類例が出土している{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳出土 琥珀製枕.JPG|琥珀製枕 ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳出土 琥珀製枕 (復元).JPG|琥珀製枕（復元） ファイル:竜田御坊山3号墳出土 三彩有蓋円面硯・ガラス管.JPG|三彩有蓋円面硯・ガラス管 == 被葬者 == 竜田御坊山3号墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、上宮王家の一族に比定する説が挙げられる。上宮王家は、厩戸皇子（[[聖徳太子]]）・[[山背大兄王]]を中心とした一族で、[[推古天皇]]13年（[[601年]]）に厩戸皇子が[[斑鳩宮]]に移住してから[[皇極天皇]]2年（[[643年]]）に山背大兄王が自害するまでの間の斑鳩地域の一大勢力とされる{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。『[[上宮聖徳法王帝説]]』では、山背大兄王の滅亡の際には王の子弟ら15人が滅ぼされたという{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 竜田御坊山3号墳を上宮王家の一族に比定する説では、横口式石槨・漆棺・副葬品に見る被葬者の身分の高さや、所在地名の「御坊山」が「御廟山」の転訛と考えられることが根拠として挙げられる{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。築造時期と人骨の年齢観からは、上宮王家一族の中でも特に厩戸皇子の孫世代の可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。ただし漆塗陶棺は急ごしらえの様相を呈することから、緊急的な埋葬に至った背景の解明が今後期待される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 斑鳩地域では、中期古墳の[[斑鳩大塚古墳]]・甲塚古墳、後期古墳の[[藤ノ木古墳]]・仏塚古墳、終末期古墳の[[神代古墳]]などが分布しており、本古墳との系譜関係が注意される{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 == 竜田御坊山1・2号墳 == 竜田御坊山1号墳・2号墳は3号墳の南西に所在する。各内容は次の通り。 ; 1号墳 : 2号墳の南15メートルに所在{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。1964年（昭和39年）に宅地造成工事に伴い破壊（調査なし）{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 : 埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]状で、長さ約2メートル・幅約1.7メートルを測ったという。石室内部からは人骨3体のほか金銅装環付六花文座金具1（木棺飾金具か）・鉄釘4が出土している。築造時期は古墳時代終末期の7世紀前半-中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 ; 2号墳 : 1号墳の北15メートル、3号墳の南西30メートルに所在{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。1964年（昭和39年）に宅地造成工事に伴い破壊（調査なし）{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 : 埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]と見られ、[[凝灰岩]]製の組合式[[家形石棺]]片が出土している。小型の石棺で、推定規模は長さ1.82メートル・幅1.15メートルを測る。築造時期は古墳時代終末期の7世紀前半-中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|絹畠歩ら|2021}}。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 大和御坊山第三号墳出土品（考古資料） - 内訳は以下。奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館保管。1981年（昭和56年）6月9日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10002|大和御坊山第三号墳出土品}}。 ** 三彩有蓋円面硯 1面 ** 管状ガラス製品 1本 ** 琥珀枕残欠 1箇分 ** 漆塗陶棺 1合 ** （附指定）横口式石槨 1基 == 関連施設 == * [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館（橿原市畝傍町） - 竜田御坊山3号墳の石槨・出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=御坊山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|御坊山古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=高島徹|authorlink=|year=1989|chapter=御坊山古墳群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|御坊山古墳群（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=絹畠歩・前田俊雄・北井利幸|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf 竜田御坊山3号墳の再検討と被葬者像]}}|date=2021|publisher=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]|journal=考古學論攷 橿原考古学研究所紀要|volume=|number=44|naid=|pages=1-18|ref={{Harvid|絹畠歩ら|2021}}}} - リンクは奈良県立橿原考古学研究所。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]|author=|year=1977|chapter=|title=竜田御坊山古墳 -付 平野塚穴山古墳-（奈良県史跡名勝天然記念物調査報告 第32冊）|publisher=奈良県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[神代古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun|竜田御坊山古墳}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10002|大和御坊山第三号墳出土品}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=竜田御坊山古墳群 |1-1=奈良県の古墳 |1-2=斑鳩町の歴史 |1-3=古墳群 }} {{デフォルトソート:たつたこほうやまさんこうふん}} So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|竜田御坊山3号墳|version=99995437|comment=Imported full ja history}}

No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|ja|斑鳩町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style stone coffin and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|竜田御坊山3号墳|version=99995437|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]

The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important designation1 of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1 of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|ja|法隆寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōryū-ji|qq=}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the stone coffin and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|竜田御坊山3号墳|version=99995437|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikaruga]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]][[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]}} [[sn:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[en:Tatsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[sn:atsuta Gobōyama 3rd Kofun]]
