Exhibit at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} Museum)}} |alternate_name = Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun | part_of = Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Ikaruga|WD=Q379233}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.61136111111111|N|135.7265361111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter 8m
Height 2.5m |burial = {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sarcophagus|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}
(Containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|Tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}) |excavated_burial goods = Human remains, amber pillow, tri-colored lidded inkstone, glass writing tube |satellite_kofun = |built = Mid-7th century |interred_persons = One young male
(Hypothesis: a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family) |mausoleum = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = excavations (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}) |notable_features = Mound no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tatsuta Goboyama
No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|en|Ikaruga, Nara|WD=Q379233|lt=Ikaruga, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|qq=}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sarcophagus|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}. The adoption of the Cave Tomb-style Sarcophagus and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Cave Tomb-style Sarcophagus|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the Sarcophagus and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|竜田御坊山3号墳|version=99995437|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] {{wikidata link|Q114042526}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the Sarcophagus and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|竜田御坊山3号墳|version=99995437|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] {{wikidata link|Q114042526}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

[[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun |image = [[File:竜田御坊山3号墳 横口式石槨.JPG|280px]]
{{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}:附 Historic Site1)
{{small|(Left: lid and base stones, right: sealing stone. Exhibit at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} Museum)}} |alternate_name = Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun | part_of = Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Ikaruga|WD=Q379233}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.61136111111111|N|135.7265361111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter 8m
Height 2.5m |burial = {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}
(Containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|Tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}) |excavated_burial goods = Human remains, amber pillow, tri-colored lidded inkstone, glass writing tube |satellite_kofun = |built = Mid-7th century |interred_persons = One young male
(Hypothesis: a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family) |mausoleum = |historic_sites = None |tangible_cultural_properties = excavations (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}) |notable_features = Mound no longer exists |map = Japan Nara |type = Round tumulus |map_dot_label = Tatsuta Goboyama
No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|en|Ikaruga, Nara|WD=Q379233|lt=Ikaruga, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|qq=}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}. The adoption of the Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the Sarcophagus and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|竜田御坊山3号墳|version=99995437|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] {{wikidata link|Q114042526}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}. The adoption of the Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji|1=Hōryū-ji}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} Museum - Houses and exhibits the Sarcophagus and burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun. == External Links == * {{National Heritage Database|201|10002|burial goods from Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun}} of Japan in Nara Prefecture]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|竜田御坊山3号墳|version=99995437|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:竜田御坊山3号墳]] {{wikidata link|Q114042526}} [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|en|Ikaruga, Nara|WD=Q379233|lt=Ikaruga, Nara|1=Ikaruga, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|qq=}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji|1=Hōryū-ji}}. No. 3 Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun''' (竜田御坊山3号墳, also called Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun) was a [[Kofun]] located in {{ill|Ikaruga, Nara|en|Ikaruga, Nara|WD=Q379233|lt=Ikaruga, Nara|1=Ikaruga, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, it was a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The mound has since disappeared, but the excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. == Overview == Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun was a small round tumulus built on the southern slope of a hill in the northwestern Nara Basin, north of {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|ja|龍田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine (Nara)|qq=}} and southwest of {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji|1=Hōryū-ji}}. It was part of the Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun Cluster, with two other tumuli (Nos. 1 and 2) located to the southwest. The tumulus was discovered during a residential development project in 1965 (Showa 40), after which it was excavated and subsequently destroyed{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been circular, with a diameter of about 8 meters and a height of 2.5 meters{{Sfn|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The burial facility was a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, opening southward and containing a lacquered house-shaped {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}. The adoption of the Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus and lacquered coffin indicates the high social status of the interred individual. However, the hastily prepared lacquered tōkan, with mismatched components and repair marks, suggests an emergency burial{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. burial goods recovered from the coffin include human remains (a 14–15-year-old male), an amber pillow, a tri-colored lidded inkstone, and a glass writing tube, all of which are rare and noteworthy. The kofun is dated to the mid-7th century, during the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. The excavated burial goods were Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1981 (Showa 56). == burial == The burial facility consisted of a {{ill|Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}, constructed with granite. It comprised three main components: a base stone, a lid stone, and a sealing stone. The area of these components are as follows{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}: * Lid stone: Maximum length 2.86 m, width 1.61 m, height 0.93 m * Base stone: Maximum length 2.93 m, width 1.67 m, height 0.57 m * Sealing stone: Maximum width 1.11 m, height 0.90 m, thickness 0.35 m The interior of the coffin was coated with plaster, enhancing its sealing capability. The lacquered tōkan inside the coffin, although of high status, showed signs of emergency preparation, with mismatched components and hasty repairs{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == Cultural burial goods == burial goods excavated from the tōkan include: * Amber pillow: Analyzed as originating from Kuji, Iwate Prefecture * Tri-colored lidded inkstone: Possibly imported from China or the Korean Peninsula * Glass writing tube: May correspond to writing tools described in Wang Xizhi’s "Pen Treatise" == Historical Context == The interred individual is hypothesized to be a member of the Upper Miyake Royal Family, descendants of Prince Shōtoku. The cluster’s proximity to key temples like {{ill|Hōryū-ji|en|Hōryū-ji|WD=Q261932|lt=Hōryū-ji|1=Hōryū-ji}} suggests a connection to the region’s political and religious developments{{Sfn|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Goboyama Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Topography Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Topography Series|qq=}} 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|Goboyama Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kinuhata Ayumu et al.|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/kiyo44-kinuhata.pdf Reassessment of Tatsuta Goboyama No. 3 Kofun and Its burial Context]}}|date=2021|journal=Kōkogaku Ronkō: Kashihara Archaeological Institute Bulletin|publisher={{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}|volume=44|pages=1–18|ref={{Harvid|Kinuhata et al.|2021}}}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} Museum -
